segments.text: Roger: Good morning.
Roger: This is the Customer Service Department of Kingswell Washing Machine.
Roger: Roger speaking, how may I help you?
Woman: Good morning.
Woman: I'm calling about the washing machine I bought from the mall several months ago.
Woman: There's something wrong with it at the moment, so what do I have to do?
Roger: OK, first I need to get a few details about this.
Roger: Could you give me your name, please?
Woman: Yes, it's Judie Smith.
Roger: OK.
Roger: I need one more thing.
Roger: There's a reference number.
Roger: It should be in the top right-hand corner of the warranty card we gave you.
Woman: Let me have a look … Oh, here it is.
Woman: It's CWX576884.
Roger: Thanks.
Roger: Well, I need to make sure if your washing machine is still within the warranty period, so when did you buy it?
Woman: Oh, sure.
Woman: I remember I bought it last summer, so … it was about 9 months ago.
Woman: And the card here says this washing machine has a 2-year warranty, so it's definitely within the period.
Roger: Of course it is, Madam.
Roger: All right, then I need your address?
Woman: My old address or my current one?
Roger: Your current one, as we will send in someone to fix the washing machine for you.
Woman: It's 120 Middle Street, Oxford.
Roger: And the postcode?
Woman: It's GO19 4KE.
Roger: Now I need to know the exact problem of the washing machine.
Roger: Could you describe it in detail?
Woman: Actually the washing machine did work very well until last weekend.
Woman: I put some clothes into it, and it worked normally at first, then I just got down to doing some gardening.
Woman: But after about 30 minutes when I came back to the house, I found it was leaking!
Woman: The water had soaked the entire floor.
Roger: I'm sorry to hear that, Madam.
Roger: So did you do anything to make the leaking stop?
Woman: At that moment, the washing machine was totally out of control, and the alarm on it also started to sound.
Woman: I thought there was not much point in pressing any button on the machine, so I just switched off the machine straight away.
Woman: Then it stopped leaking at once, thank goodness!
Roger: Well, I think that was the only thing you could do.
Woman: Oh then, I found my floor was nearly a disaster.
Woman: As it is made of wood rather than marble, it could not be soaked in water for a long time, so what I did in the next 2 hours was wiping up the water on the floor!
Roger: Right.
Woman: Later I tried the washing machine once more, but the problem still existed.
Roger: Well, I've taken down all the related information.
Roger: Later we'll send a client engineer to your house to fix it.
Woman: OK.
Woman: Could you arrange the repair work as soon as possible, as I'm too busy at work to do the laundry by hand?
Woman: How about this Saturday?
Roger: Umm … I have to check the schedule of the repairman … Oh sorry, Madam, Saturday has been occupied.
Roger: The earliest day we can arrange for you is next Tuesday.
Roger: Is that OK with you?
Woman: Fine, I can take a leave from work that day.
Woman: Anyway, thanks.
Woman: Oh, my house is easy to find.
Woman: It's near the Central Market, just across from the Post Office.
Roger: All right.
Roger: And the last thing, could you tell me your phone number, please?
Woman: Sure.
Woman: It's 3864 7757.
Roger: OK.
Roger: I've got it.
Roger: The repairman will contact you before he comes.
Woman: OK.
Woman: Thank you very much.
Roger: You're welcome.
Roger: Bye!
Woman: Bye!
